title: arabis perennans var. longipes
intro: Arabis perennans var. longipes, also known as Long-stalked Rockcress, is a perennial plant species in the mustard family, characterized by its tall stalks and white, four-petaled flowers. The plant is used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally employed to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Arabis perennans var. longipes are believed to provide numerous health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Arabis perennans var. longipes can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
